_patch_meta.description: Dimension review patch — C21: transformation / renewal / blindness (spiritual) / deadness. 46 groups updated; 3 Session B findings; 1 Session D pointer; 4 registry stamps; 1 cluster stamp. v2: Session B/D insert operations corrected to match table schemas.

set.notes: anakainōsis — renewal of the mind by the Spirit as ground for discerning God's will. KEYWORD_WEAK confirmed.
cha.li.phah — change as inner transformation; hoped-for renewal or persistent hardness. KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed.
anakainoō — progressive inner renewal toward God's image. Automated Cognition label incorrect; renewal process is the primary characteristic. Corrected to Transformation.
ma.cha.la.phah — Nazirite locks as physical expression of inner covenantal empowerment; cutting = loss of divine-given capacity. Agency/Power confirmed.

cha.loph — the perishing call forth the conscience and advocacy-will of others (Prov 31:8). Inner-being characteristic is the advocate's moral conscience. Moral Character confirmed. Cluster assignment retained per researcher guidance: cluster is a processing unit.
metamorfoō — inner transformation toward conformity with God, renewal of mind (Rom 12:2). KEYWORD_STRONG confirmed.
metamorfoō — Christ's Transfiguration mediates human inner encounter with divine reality. Subject of transformation is Christ. Transformation confirmed; dominant subject GOD.
cha.laph — inner renewal of strength through waiting on God (Isa 40:31). Automated Vitality/Existence label corrected: renewal of vitality is Transformation, not static condition.
cha.laph — transience of human life; rapid passing of creaturely existence (Psa 90:5). Vitality/Existence confirmed.
cha.laph — wilful overriding of divine covenant; inner act of defiance against binding obligation (Isa 24:5). Automated Divine-Human Correspondence label incorrect. Corrected to Volition.
cha.laph — divine spirit passing near, engaging inner perception or producing inner dread (Job 9:11). Emotion — Negative confirmed.
morfoō — interior formation of Christ within the person; inner shaping toward Christ-likeness (Gal 4:19). Transformation confirmed.

skotoō — divine judgment plunging domain into darkness; human inner anguish produced (Rev 16:10). Automated Divine-Human Correspondence label corrected: anguish is the inner-being characteristic. Corrected to Emotion — Negative.
tuflos — spiritual blindness as inner condition: self-deception, hardness, inability to perceive spiritual reality (John 9:41). UNCLASSIFIED corrected to Cognition.

tuflos — physical blindness as condition of need healed by Messiah; faith and spiritual perception called forth as inner-being content (Luke 4:18). Retained per inclusion principle. UNCLASSIFIED corrected to Cognition.

skotos — darkness as the inner-spiritual domain defining the person's fundamental identity and condition (Eph 5:8: you were darkness, now you are light). Automated Cognition label corrected to Identity/Selfhood.
skotos — darkness as moral domain of works and powers; participation in or renunciation of (Rom 13:12). Moral Character confirmed.
skotos — darkness of the inner life: hidden purposes, corrupted inner light, self-deception (1Cor 4:5). Automated Volition label corrected: hidden inner knowing is Cognition.
skotos — outer darkness of eschatological judgment; ultimate condition of exclusion from God (Matt 22:13). UNCLASSIFIED corrected to Vitality/Existence.
skotia — darkness as constitutive inner condition of spiritual alienation: hating, not knowing direction, blinded (1 John 2:11). UNCLASSIFIED corrected to Identity/Selfhood.
skotia — darkness of concealment; inner life hidden in secret to be brought to light (Luke 12:3). Automated Vitality/Existence label corrected to Cognition.
skotizō — darkening of inner capacities: understanding, heart, eyes; consequence of wilful rejection or divine judicial act (Eph 4:18). Cognition confirmed.
skoteinos — inner darkness filling the person when orientation is corrupted; whole inner life darkened by bad eye (Matt 6:23). Vitality/Existence confirmed.

tufloō — blinding of inner capacities: eyes and mind; agents include hatred, adversary, divine judgment (2Cor 4:4). Automated Divine-Human Correspondence label corrected: cognitive incapacity is the characteristic. Corrected to Cognition.
nekros — spiritual deadness as the person's condition: dead in sin, dead to God (Eph 2:1). ROOT_INFERRED Vitality/Existence confirmed.

nekros — dead to sin / dead to the law; inner reorientation through dying with Christ (Rom 6:11; 7:4). ROOT_INFERRED Vitality/Existence corrected: dying-to is transformative reorientation, not static death-condition. Corrected to Transformation.
nekros — dead works arising from spiritual deadness; conscience purified from them (Heb 9:14). Moral Character confirmed.
nekros — dead faith: faith as inner capacity that can be alive or dead (Jas 2:17). Automated Agency/Power label corrected: vitality of faith as capacity is Vitality/Existence.
nekros — resurrection of the dead as ground for inner hope, faith, aspiration (Phil 3:11; 1 Pet 1:3). Vitality/Existence confirmed.
mut — death as consequence of inner moral failure: folly, wickedness, sin (Gen 2:17; Job 5:2). Moral Character confirmed.
mut — death as object of inner orientation: fear, bitterness, longing, despair (Job 21:25; Jon 4:8). Emotion — Negative confirmed.

mut — God's sovereignty over death as ground of inner trust and confidence (1 Sam 2:6; Hab 1:12). Automated Agency/Power label corrected: human inner posture is trust/dependence. Corrected to Dependence/Creatureliness.
mut — the dead cut off from God's hand; loss of praise capacity (Psa 88:5, 10). Automated Agency/Power label corrected: relational severance and incapacity of the dead is Vitality/Existence.
thnēskō — spiritual deadness in a living person: physically alive but inwardly dead through self-indulgence (1 Tim 5:6). UNCLASSIFIED corrected to Vitality/Existence.

nekroō — deliberate mortification of inner capacities: wilfully putting to death what is earthly (Col 3:5). Automated Vitality/Existence label corrected: mortification is a volitional act. Corrected to Volition.
ma.vet — death as end of a moral path; choosing life vs death (Deu 30:19; Pro 8:36). Volition confirmed.
ma.vet — death as power/threat the person faces, fears, is delivered from (1 Sam 20:3; Psa 55:4). Emotion — Negative confirmed.
ma.vet — God's sovereignty over death as ground of inner redemption and hope (Isa 25:8; Hos 13:14). Parallel to 1379-003. Automated Agency/Power label corrected to Dependence/Creatureliness.

ma.vet — the dead and inner-being capacities: no longer praising, remembering, or remembered (Psa 6:5; Psa 116:15). UNCLASSIFIED corrected to Vitality/Existence. Session B pointer DIM-210-001 raised on verse grouping tension.

thanatos — death as spiritual/moral condition: separation from God, consequence of sin (Rom 8:6; 1 John 3:14). Automated Moral Character label corrected: being-in-death as condition is Vitality/Existence.
thanatos — death as enslaving power through fear; lifelong slavery through fear of death (Heb 2:15). Emotion — Negative confirmed.
thanatos — believer's inner identification with Christ's death as transformative reality (Phil 3:10). Automated Vitality/Existence label corrected: participatory dying-with-Christ is Transformation.

thanatos — faithful inner orientation toward death: perseverance, defiance, hope (Rev 2:10; 1 Cor 15:55). Automated Vitality/Existence label corrected: faithfulness-unto-death is a volitional posture. Corrected to Volition.
thanatos — extreme inner anguish at threshold of death: soul sorrowful even to death (Mark 14:34, Gethsemane). Subject is Christ. Emotion — Negative confirmed.

thnētos — mortal body as site of inner orientation, longing, moral struggle (Rom 6:12; 2 Cor 5:4). Automated Volition label corrected: mortal embodiment as constitutive condition is Vitality/Existence.

sunapothnēskō — inner commitment of dying-together; covenantal loyalty through shared death including Christ (2 Cor 7:3; 2 Tim 2:11). Automated Vitality/Existence label corrected: relational inner orientation of loyalty is Relational Disposition.

operations.description: Registry 134 (renewal): Dimension Review complete under v1.8
Registry 202 (transformation): Dimension Review complete under v1.8
Registry 207 (blindness (spiritual)): Dimension Review complete under v1.8
Registry 210 (deadness): Dimension Review complete under v1.8

record.notes: Transformation/renewal pole (Regs 134, 202) and deadness/blindness pole (Regs 207, 210). Key pattern: death/darkness vocabulary spans multiple dimensions — dual-register flagged for Session B. One cluster-fit anomaly (6136-001 cha.loph) retained per researcher guidance: cluster is processing unit only.

operations.description: Cluster C21 Dimension Review complete — stamp set

record.finding: Group 7423-004 (H4194 ma.vet) contains two anchor verses in potential tension: Psa 6:5 (in death there is no remembrance of you; in Sheol who will give you praise — incapacity of the dead) and Psa 116:15 (Precious in the sight of the Lord is the death of his saints — God's relational valuation of the dying). The group description addresses the incapacity reading only. Session B should assess whether these two verses represent a coherent group or whether 7423-004 should be subdivided: one group naming the incapacity of the dead, one naming God's regard for the death of the faithful.

operations.description: DIM-210-001: Session B finding — deadness: group 7423-004 anchor verse tension

record.finding: The deadness vocabulary (nekros, thanatos, ma.vet, mut) generates a consistent dual-register pattern: death as condition (Vitality/Existence) and death as moral/relational reality (Moral Character, Transformation, Relational Disposition). The same root terms name both the physical/existential fact of death and the spiritual/moral state it represents. Session B should map this dual-register explicitly — particularly the distinction between dead as condition you are in (nekros 1378-001, 1378-005; thanatos 7427-001) versus dead as act you perform or participate in (nekros 1378-002; nekroō 7422-001; thanatos 7427-003).

operations.description: DIM-210-002: Session B finding — deadness: dual-register pattern across death vocabulary

record.finding: The darkness vocabulary (skotos, skotia, skoteinos, skotizō, skotoō) generates multiple distinct inner-being characteristics across its groups: identity condition (7432-001, 7433-001), moral domain (7432-002), concealment/cognition (7432-003, 7433-002), eschatological consequence (7432-004), whole-person darkening (7435-001), and active darkening of faculties (7434-001). This is a term family with unusually wide inner-being range. Session B should assess whether these groups reflect a coherent single inner-being phenomenon (darkness as multi-dimensional spiritual reality) or whether the groupings require further subdivision.

operations.description: DIM-207-001: Session B finding — blindness (spiritual): darkness vocabulary wide inner-being range

record.description: C21 contains a structural pairing: blindness/darkness vocabulary (Reg 207) and transformation/renewal vocabulary (Regs 134, 202) describe opposite inner-being poles — the darkened/blinded/dead person vs. the renewed/transformed/living person. The same dimension (Transformation) appears in Regs 134, 202 and also in Reg 210 (1378-002 dead to sin; 7427-003 dying with Christ). Session D should examine whether transformation vocabulary and death-and-resurrection vocabulary in C21 describe the same underlying inner-being phenomenon (inner renewal as death-and-new-life) or distinct phenomena.

operations.description: DIM-207-SD001: Session D pointer — C21 structural pairing: blindness/darkness vs transformation/renewal
